title: Global Startup Funding: AI Dominates Early February 2026 - LinkedIn
full_text:
🚀 Global Startup Funding Insights — Early February 2026
🚀 Global Startup Funding Insights — Early February 2026
The first week of February continues to highlight AI as the dominant driver of venture funding, while strategic bets in climate, energy, and Web3 remain active. Global VC activity surged with notable rounds and unicorns emerging across the U.S., Europe, and Asia.
🔹 Key Highlights (Last Week)
Resolve AI | AI / Enterprise | Series A | US$1B valuation
Closed US$125M; reflects investor appetite for production-ready AI infrastructure. (https://lnkd.in/g34kiSD8)
Goodfire | AI Research | Series B | US$1.25B valuation
Raised US$150M; signals strong interest in AI interpretability platforms. (https://lnkd.in/g34kiSD8)
TRM Labs | AI / Security | Series C | US$70M
Blockchain/crypto security tools remain attractive to investors. (https://lnkd.in/g34kiSD8)
Urban SDK | Gov-Tech / AI | Growth Round | US$65M
AI adoption in public sector applications gains momentum. (https://lnkd.in/g34kiSD8)
Lunar Energy | Climate / Energy | Series D | US$232M
Funding for home battery and virtual power plant software expansion. (https://lnkd.in/g34kiSD8)
Plug | EV Marketplace | Series A | Lightspeed | US$20M
Highlights growth-stage interest in mobility and marketplace ecosystems. (https://lnkd.in/g34kiSD8)
Fibr AI | Martech / AI | Seed | ~US$7.5M
Early-stage conviction in agentic web experience layers. (https://lnkd.in/g34kiSD8)
📈 Trends & Insights
AI remains king: Late-stage and growth rounds dominate, particularly enterprise-grade infrastructure (https://lnkd.in/gfZv6xEv).
Diversification beyond AI: Climate tech, governance tech, and mobility attract strategic capital.
Investor confidence rising: Large Series B/C rounds reflect improved sentiment in high-growth sectors.
🧭 Strategic Takeaways
Opportunity Areas: Enterprise AI deployment, climate/energy software, and Web3 security & compliance.
Risks: Early-stage funding remains selective; regulatory pressures in fintech & crypto persist.
Actionables: Track startups moving beyond LLM focus toward enterprise-grade AI and cross-sector applications.
💡 Key Insight: February confirms AI-led innovation drives valuations, but the next growth wave may come from startups combining AI with energy, mobility, and governance solutions.
Here are the 55 US AI startups that raised $100M or more in 2025 | TechCrunch
